source: ICAR eCourse + NIPHM IPM Packages + MPKV Rahuri + VNMKV Parbhani

varieties.notes: JS 335 is most popular but susceptible to Yellow Mosaic Virus. KDS 726 has better YMV resistance.

fertilizer.basal: Urea 43 kg + SSP 625 kg + MOP 33 kg per hectare
fertilizer.notes: Soybean fixes its own nitrogen. Excess N causes vegetative growth.

agronomy.irrigation: Critical stages: flowering (30-35 DAS) and pod filling (50-65 DAS). Rain-fed crop, 3-4 irrigations if needed.
agronomy.yield: 15-25 quintal/ha average, up to 35 with good management

yellow_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Yellow-green mosaic pattern on leaves, stunted growth, reduced pods
management: Use resistant variety KDS 726 or MAUS 81
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Control whitefly vector: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 5ml/15L water
Yellow sticky traps @ 5/acre to monitor whitefly
yellow_mosaic_virus.severity: Can cause 40-100% yield loss in severe cases

charcoal_rot.symptoms: Wilting at pod filling stage, grey-black discoloration inside stem, tiny black sclerotia inside stem
charcoal_rot.cause: Fungus Macrophomina phaseolina - worsened by drought stress
management: Avoid water stress during flowering and pod filling
Crop rotation with non-host crops (jowar, wheat)

bacterial_pustule.symptoms: Small yellow spots with raised center (pustule) on both leaf surfaces
management: Streptomycin 0.5g + Copper oxychloride 3g per liter spray

stem_fly.symptoms: Yellow cotyledon leaves, wilting seedlings, white larvae inside stem
management: Seed treatment: Imidacloprid 70 WS @ 7g/kg seed (most effective)

girdle_beetle.symptoms: Two circular rings cut on stem at 10-15 cm height, upper portion dries
management: Collect and destroy damaged stem pieces

spodoptera.symptoms: Skeletonized leaves first, then complete defoliation, group feeding initially
spodoptera.etl: 2 egg masses or 4 larvae per meter row
management: Collect and destroy egg masses and young larvae

harvest.timing: When 95% pods turn brown, leaves drop (90-100 DAS)
harvest.moisture: Harvest when seed moisture 18-20%, dry to 12% for storage
harvest.storage: Cool dry place, moisture below 12%, use hermetic bags

varieties.notes: All commercial cotton in Maharashtra is Bt hybrid. Bollworm resistance built-in but secondary pests now major issue.

fertilizer.schedule: Basal: Full P+K + 30kg N. 30 DAS: 30kg N. 60 DAS: 30kg N. 90 DAS: 30kg N
fertilizer.micronutrients: Ferrous sulphate 10kg/acre for reddening of leaves

agronomy.irrigation: Critical: germination, squaring (45 DAS), flowering (60-75 DAS), boll development (75-110 DAS)

pink_bollworm.symptoms: Rosette flowers (लाडू फूल), exit holes on bolls, damaged seeds inside
pink_bollworm.etl: 8-10% rosette flowers OR 5-8% infested green bolls
management: Pheromone trap 5/acre from 45 DAS for monitoring
Summer deep ploughing to destroy pupae

whitefly.symptoms: Yellow leaves, honeydew + sooty mold on leaves, tiny white flies under leaf
whitefly.etl: 10 adults per leaf average over 5 plants
management: Avoid broad-spectrum pesticides that kill natural enemies
Rotate insecticides - do not use same twice

bacterial_blight.symptoms: Angular water-soaked spots on leaves turning brown, water-soaked stem lesions
management: Seed treatment: Streptomycin 0.1g + Copper oxychloride 3g/kg seed
Remove and destroy severely infected plants

fusarium_wilt.symptoms: Sudden wilting, yellowing of leaves, brown discoloration inside stem when cut
fusarium_wilt.cause: Fungus Fusarium oxysporum f.sp. vasinfectum - soil-borne
management: Soil application of Trichoderma 2.5kg/acre in FYM
Crop rotation with non-host crops for 2-3 years

agronomy.seed_treatment: Aregano 3% or Carbendazim 0.1% + Chlorpyriphos 0.1% for 10 min dip

fertilizer.schedule: Split N in 4 doses: planting, 30, 90, 150 DAS

agronomy.duration: Suru: 14-15 months. Adsali: 18 months. Pre-season: 12 months.
agronomy.yield: 70-100 tons/ha, up to 120 with drip irrigation

top_borer.symptoms: Dead heart (central shoot dries in young crop), window pane feeding on leaves

woolly_aphid.symptoms: White woolly masses on stem near leaf axils, yellowing, stunting

red_rot.symptoms: Red discoloration with white patches inside stem when cut, drying of top leaves
management: Sett treatment in Carbendazim 0.1% for 30 min

agronomy.fertilizer: N:P:K = 25:50:25 kg/ha at sowing only (no top-dressing)

fusarium_wilt.symptoms: Sudden wilting of whole plant, brown/black discoloration inside root and stem
management: Use resistant variety BSMR 736 or BSMR 853
Soil application of Trichoderma 2.5kg/acre mixed with FYM
Crop rotation: avoid tur in same field 3 consecutive years

sterility_mosaic.symptoms: Green mosaic on leaves, no flowers or pods, excessive vegetative growth
management: Remove and destroy infected plants early
Spray Dicofol 18.5 EC @ 20ml/15L to control mite vector

gram_pod_borer.symptoms: Round holes in pods, caterpillar inside pod eating seeds
management: Neem oil 5% spray at early infestation
Spray in evening hours when larvae are active

agronomy.sowing_time: November 1-30 (timely sown). After Dec 15 = late sown.

fertilizer.schedule: Half N + full P + full K at sowing. Remaining N at first irrigation (CRI stage, 20-25 DAS)

agronomy.irrigation: CRI (20-25 DAS), tillering (40-45 DAS), jointing (60-65 DAS), flowering (80-85 DAS), grain filling (100-105 DAS)

yellow_rust.symptoms: Yellow-orange pustules in stripes on leaves, powdery yellow spores
yellow_rust.cause: Fungus Puccinia striiformis - spreads in cool humid weather
management: Two sprays 15 days apart if severe

loose_smut.symptoms: Black powdery mass replaces grain in ear head, smut spores released at heading
loose_smut.cause: Fungus Ustilago segetum var. tritici - seed-borne
management: Seed treatment with Carboxin + Thiram before sowing - only prevention
No cure once infected - remove infected heads before spore release

aphid.symptoms: Green/yellow insects on ear heads and upper leaves, sticky honeydew
aphid.etl: 5 aphids per ear head at grain filling
management: Avoid spraying when crop is flowering

agronomy.nursery: Raise nursery 40-45 days before transplanting. Seed rate 8-10 kg/ha.
agronomy.fertilizer: N:P:K = 100:50:50 kg/ha. Split N: 50% basal + 25% at 30 DAT + 25% at 45 DAT
agronomy.irrigation: Every 7-10 days. Stop irrigation 15 days before harvest.

purple_blotch.symptoms: Small white spots with purple center on leaves, tip dieback in severe cases
purple_blotch.cause: Fungus Alternaria porri - worsened in humid conditions

thrips.symptoms: Silver-white patches on leaves, distorted growth, tiny insects visible
management: Spray in evening, directed to leaf axils

basal_rot.symptoms: Yellowing of outer leaves, soft rotting at base of bulb, pink-white mycelium
management: Seedling treatment: Carbendazim 2g/L for 30 min dip before transplanting
Soil drench with Carbendazim 1g/L around base

april_pruning.activity: Prune to 4-6 buds on fruiting canes

april_to_october_management.april_may: Training new shoots, remove weak/diseased canes, start fungicide spray program
april_to_october_management.june_july: Monsoon disease management critical - Downy mildew pressure high. Reduce irrigation. Remove dense canopy.
april_to_october_management.august: Reduce N application, balance vegetative-reproductive growth
april_to_october_management.september: Cane maturity, prepare for October pruning. Apply potassium for wood ripening.
april_to_october_management.october_pruning: Main fruiting pruning. Prune to 6-8 eye canes.

spray_schedule_april_to_october: April-May: Bordeaux mixture 1% every 15 days for Downy Mildew prevention
June-July: Ridomil Gold (Metalaxyl+Mancozeb) 2.5g/L every 10 days during monsoon
July-Aug: Fosetyl-Al 3g/L systemic fungicide for DM
Aug-Sep: Hexaconazole 5 EC @ 10ml/15L for powdery mildew
September: Stop fungicide 30 days before pruning

downy_mildew.symptoms: Yellow oily spots on upper leaf, white downy growth on underside, shoot tip curling
downy_mildew.cause: Oomycete Plasmopara viticola - favored by rain and humidity
management: Bordeaux mixture 1% (1kg CuSO4 + 1kg lime per 100L) preventive spray
Spray both upper and lower leaf surface

powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, shoots, berries. Berries crack.
management: Do not spray sulfur above 35°C temperature

anthracnose.symptoms: Circular dark spots on leaves and berries with light center
management: Remove and destroy infected plant parts

fertilizer_schedule.post_april_pruning: Apply FYM 40 t/ha + Single Super Phosphate 300 kg/ha + MOP 200 kg/ha
fertilizer_schedule.august_september: 50 kg MOP/ha for cane maturity and sugar accumulation

varieties.best_maharashtra: Bhagwa - thick skin, dark red arils, good shelf life

bacterial_blight_telya.symptoms: Water-soaked oily patches on leaves turning dark, spots on fruits, fruit cracking
management: Remove and destroy infected plant parts
Spray at 15 day interval during rain
bacterial_blight_telya.note: Major disease in Maharashtra - responsible for heavy losses

fruit_borer.symptoms: Entry hole in fruit, frass visible, internal damage, premature fruit drop
management: Fruit bagging with paper bags at marble stage
Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 3ml/15L spray at petal fall

agronomy.fertilizer: N:P:K = 80:40:40 kg/ha. Half N at sowing, half at knee height stage.

shoot_fly.symptoms: Dead heart - central shoot turns yellow and dies, can be pulled out easily
management: Early sowing to escape peak fly period

grain_mold.symptoms: Pink, red, black discoloration on grains during grain filling
grain_mold.cause: Complex of fungi Fusarium, Curvularia, Colletotrichum
management: Use resistant varieties Phule Vasudha, CSR 3
Spray Mancozeb 75 WP @ 30g/15L at grain filling
Harvest at proper maturity, avoid rain at harvest

urea.application: Split doses, never apply before rain forecast

single_super_phosphate.advantage: Contains sulfur - good for oilseeds like soybean

mop.use: For fruit crops and root crops

micronutrients.zinc_deficiency: Symptoms: White striping on young leaves. Apply ZnSO4 25 kg/ha basal or 5g/L foliar.
micronutrients.iron_deficiency: Symptoms: Yellowing between veins on young leaves. Apply FeSO4 10 kg/ha or spray 1%.
micronutrients.boron_deficiency: Symptoms: Hollow stem in onion, poor fruit set. Borax 10 kg/ha or 0.2% spray.

safety_rules: Always wear mask, gloves, full sleeve shirt while spraying
Wash hands and face after spraying
Do not spray during flowering (honey bee protection)
Do not spray at midday in summer - spray early morning or evening
pesticide_safety.first_aid_pesticide_poisoning: Remove from exposure, wash with water, go to nearest hospital immediately. Poison Control: 1800-116-117

pH.alkaline_above_8: Apply gypsum 2-4 t/ha or sulfur 200 kg/ha

organic_farming_inputs.jeevamrit: 10 kg desi cow dung + 10 L desi cow urine + 2 kg besan + 1 kg jaggery + 1 handful soil from tree base in 200L water. Apply after 7 days fermentation @ 200L/acre.
organic_farming_inputs.dashparni_kadha: Neem + tobacco + chilli + garlic extract. Natural pesticide.
organic_farming_inputs.ghana_jeevamrit: Solid form of jeevamrit for soil application.
